home.metaTitle: OpenClienting — The open venture clienting knowledge base
home.metaDescription: Peer-validated problem templates and pilot playbooks from SMEs. Pre-validated demand for startups. The open venture clienting knowledge base.
home.heroEyebrow: The open venture clienting knowledge base
home.heroSmeSubtitle: Peer-validated problem descriptions, requirements, and pilot playbooks from SMEs who've already been stuck on it.
home.heroStartupSubtitle: Every problem here was written by a real company. Position against published requirements and earn verified success badges.
home.exploreByIndustrySubtitle: Find real challenges specific to your sector.
home.featuredSuccessStorySectionSubtitle: A recent pilot that hit its success criteria.
home.forSmesDescription: Stop drafting RFPs from scratch. Peer-validated problem templates with clear requirements, KPIs, and pilot frameworks — contributed by SMEs who've already been stuck on the same thing.
home.forSmesFeature1Body: Peer-validated problem descriptions, requirements, and success criteria you can adapt to your context.
home.forSmesFeature2Body: Publish sensitive internal pain points without exposing your company — identity stays on our side for moderation only.
home.forSmesFeature3Body: See which problems peers in your industry are also stuck on, and which pilots actually closed.
home.forStartupsDescription: Every published problem was written by a real SME. Skip cold outreach — position against explicit requirements and earn verified success badges when your pilot lands.
home.forStartupsFeature1Body: Each problem includes upvote counts, peer-submitted requirements, and industry context — PMF signal before you build.
home.forStartupsFeature2Body: When a pilot closes, the SME files a moderated success report. The badge follows your solution across the site.
home.forStartupsFeature3Body: Meet prospects after they've written down the pain themselves, not before.
home.howItWorksSubtitle: From problem discovery to successful pilots — in three steps.
home.step1Body: Explore validated business challenges from SMEs across industries, complete with requirements and success criteria.
home.step2Body: Submit problem templates, add requirements, propose pilot frameworks, and share your domain expertise.
home.step3Body: Use structured pilot playbooks to test solutions. Report success stories and help the community learn.
home.recentProblemsSubtitle: Latest challenges submitted by the community.

ventureClienting.metaDescription: Venture clienting is a way for SMEs to de-risk innovation by piloting startup solutions against explicit problems, instead of investing equity. OpenClienting is the open knowledge base for it.
ventureClienting.title: What is venture clienting?
ventureClienting.lede: Venture clienting is a way for established companies to work with startups without taking equity — by becoming their first real customer. OpenClienting is the open, crowdsourced knowledge base behind it.
ventureClienting.section1Body: Instead of writing a check, the company writes a problem. A startup pitches against that problem with a concrete pilot. If the pilot hits its success criteria, the company becomes a paying customer. No cap table, no board seat, no multi-year negotiation — just demand meeting supply around a well-defined challenge.
ventureClienting.section2Title: How is it different from corporate venture capital?
ventureClienting.section2Body: Corporate VC buys equity and hopes for financial return. Venture clienting buys an outcome — a working pilot — and hopes for operational return. It's faster, cheaper for the company, and usually more valuable for the startup, because a reference customer is worth more than a small check.
ventureClienting.section3Title: What SMEs get out of it
ventureClienting.section3Body: A published problem is a filter. Instead of 50 cold pitches a month, you get startups self-qualifying against requirements you wrote yourself. Reuse peer-validated templates, KPIs, and pilot frameworks from SMEs who've already been stuck on the same thing — and publish anonymously if the pain is sensitive.
ventureClienting.section4Title: What startups get out of it
ventureClienting.section4Body: Every problem on the site is a real SME who has explicitly written down the pain. No guessing at product-market fit, no cold outreach, no gatekeepers. Propose a pilot against an explicit set of requirements, and — when it lands — earn a verified success badge that follows your solution across the platform.
ventureClienting.section5Body: Most venture clienting programs today are closed inside big-company innovation teams. The templates, requirements, and pilot playbooks they build get locked behind NDAs and never benefit anyone else. OpenClienting is the opposite bet: make the knowledge base open, let smaller SMEs reuse it, and let startups see demand they'd otherwise never hear about.
